calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff'S Application To Amend Abstract Of Judgment With Defendant'S Alias (Ccp Sec 680.135)
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MERIWEST CREDIT UNION'S Motion And Plaintiff'S Application To Amend Abstract Of Judgment With Defendant'S Alias (Ccp Sec 680.135).

Plaintiff Meriwest Credit Union's Motion And Plaintiff's Application To Amend Abstract Of Judgment With Defendant's Alias (CCP Sec 680.135), for which no opposition has been filed, is DENIED. Plaintiff seeks to amend the abstract of judgment to add "Light Mary J Trust," which plaintiff identifies as a revocable trust. Section 680.135 is limited to adding to judgments "aliases" of known debtors. In this context, an alias is a name by which the natural person who is the debtor is or has been known and may own property. "Light Mary J Trust" refers to a revocable trust, not a person, and cannot own property. Nor has Plaintiff identified any authority providing the court may or should treat the trust as an "alias." In any event, on this record the court doesn't know the basics of the Trust, including, for example, who the settlor(s) is/are, who the trustee(s) is/are, who the beneficiaries are or the nature or extent of any beneficiary's interest. It is worth noting section 709.010 of the Code of Civil Procedure and related law seem to provide a procedure by which a creditor may seeks to collect on a judgment from a debtor's beneficial interest in a trust.

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeats the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
